reviews.review_msg: I took her Ling100 course originally and it went well, so I decided to take another course with her. Apparently that was a mistake, as all of our lectures were prerecorded from the semester before, there was barely any communication and she would constantly be regrading tests and midterms. I had to learn everything on my own. Don't recommend

Weekly quizzes and 3 midterms. As others have mentioned, often the questions on the tests were incorrect, and she would fix them afterwards and give you credit, but it made the tests stressful. Lectures were fun & engaging, she is friendly & helpful. Answered all questions asked during lecture or on canvas. Had to study and read the book to do well
Her lectures were long but it wasn't the worst. She did have a problem with updating practice questions to the correct answer for some reason. Honestly just not the best or worst prof I've had.

Consistently remarks work because she doesn't know the correct answers for the tests SHE wrote. Tests are brutal and often make no sense, material lifted directly from the notes will be listed as an answer yet marked incorrect?? An infuriating environment that makes you want to retreat, not grow.

Trude is a nice prof, but the worst at the same time. If there was a question on the exam many students think were "unfair or marked wrong", she'll give the point. But if you got it right the first time, then you'll lose a mark when it is re-graded. Your mark will suffer with her!

I loved Dr. Heift! She is so sweet and caring. She provides clear/detailed responses to questions and spends a lot of time going over the exams explaining the answers. The class has a quiz each week, so make sure you attend and pay attention. You have to study a lot to do well in this course and the material gets harder as the semester goes on!

Very understanding caring professor. She goes over exams and explains the questions well. She wants her students to succeed and makes room for her students to explain why the question may have been confusing or too hard.

Love this prof. Had her in Fall2019 for LING100, and I loved her so much it's the only reason why I took 220. She does weekly quizzes (open book) and a participation question to make sure you don't fall behind (drop's lowest mark for each). Also, weekly homework marked by completion. She is extremely accessible, and answers questions quickly online

Weekly quizzes so class is essentially mandatory. 3 Midterms with no final, each with a group portion to boost your grade. Very kind and attentive professor that genuinely wants you to learn. Ask questions and she will help! Some of the questions on exams were unclear and were omitted. Quizzes were out of 10. Nicest teacher I've had at SFU!

I decided to take her LING100 class in Vancouver rather than in Burnaby (with a not-so-good prof), and I don't regret it at all! She does weekly tests and homework so I recommend paying attention. She drops the worst test mark though! Exams are quite hard but she always gives study outlines and no surprises. Easy A if you study hard & don't skip!

One of the best professor at SFU. Her lectures are very organized. I really get to know the materials. Another professor has taught me related knowledge as well, but he made me confused. The tests are hard but fair if you read the textbooks and pay attention to the lectures.
The best prof I've ever had! Her lectures show how passionate she is about what she teaches and she makes it easier for students to learn with easy weekly exercises in and out of class!

Shes really passionate about linguistics and had a great TA who was too. The lectures were difficult but they were always there to help. I could have skipped every week but she was very entertaining (and really cute I have a crush). Exams are fair but the final had some really hard multiple choice questions. Course was lit.

Maybe the best prof at SFU. She would remember almost every student in a big lecture! She'd even ask how you're finding things etc. She truly cares if you understand and do well (even if you weren't a great student she'd remember you and care). Somehow made a big lecture feel like a small one, not to mention she *really* knows her stuff
Trude is one of the best profs I've had at sfu. Her lectures are very organized and very entertaining.

Trude is very to the point but very approachable and helpful when you ask for it. It's easy to get the participation and homework marks if you just do it and show up to class. The exams weren't super difficult but were weighted very heavily.

Such a great prof! This course isn't easy and there is a lot of material to learn, but she is fantastic at teaching and really cares that everyone understands. Midterm and final are not easy, but if you attend lectures and do the readings you will be fine. I hope I will have an opportunity to take another class with her!

Trude is a good lecturer. She is very knowledgeable and tries really hard to make sure we understand the material. TAs were also very nice and they answered questions very very quickly on canvas. The final though was BRUTAL. I have had MATH and MACM finals that were easier!!!!!
Super clear. Super helpful. Good speaker. Awesome all around :)

One of my favourite profs at SFU. She is very knowledgeable and cares that you understand the material, but she is not an easy prof. This course may challenge you, but as long as you read the textbook and attend lectures you should do well. She's quite entertaining and has a good sense of humour as well.
Rude to students, super condescending just because people don't like to do public speaking

What is there to say? She is fantastic. Best prof I've ever had. Cares about the students and gives useful feedback while ensuring you get your stuff done. She is also extremely knowledgeable  in SLA. I think she is German. True gem, this woman.

Trude is a brilliant professor/person. She is really passionate about her profession and wants her students to succeed, but she also wants students to get the mark they deserve in terms of putting in time and effort. I missed 3 classes and it really took a toll on my grade. I suggest always being present and you will get a really good grade!
Classes are long, but content is interesting.  The tests are difficult, especially if studying is last minute.  Marking is fair.  Super kind and helpful.
Extremely nice prof. Attend every lecture, though it can get quite boring. Study her notes like crazy, and you'll get an A for sure.
she's nice & awesome. the class is easy at the beginning, the later the more interesting. It's absolutely worth it if you do your assignment and ask questions you'll be all right :)

Trudy is f*ing awesome, my buddy recommended her as an elective credit and it was totally worth it. Pretty easy at the beginning and decently interesting. The book is alright, and the assignments are cake. I had her as my TA and she was very helpful; she can come across as stern but she is really a sweetheart.

Pros - Her lecture notes were copied and pasted from the textbooks. Her exams were very difficult. Cons - She does know a lot about the topic and her classes are fun and engaging. She is nice and available for help.
super nice prof!!!And the course wasnt that hard!!good notes!!

best prof ever =) I would take any class she taught, regardless of whether or not i was interested in the subject, just because she was teaching it. Very good at explaining concepts, and also very patient when answering questions.

WOW. BEST LING PROF EVVVER. the way she teaches is very engaging. you will NEVER be bored in class. crystal clear voice. VERY helpful. nice and best of all she doesn't surprise you on the exams. one question is..why isn't she teaching other ling courses? what a waste. seriously. LOVEEE HERRR and she's funny.

One of the best profs for linguistics department.  She teaches really well in class, allowing you to be able to understand EVERYTHING just by paying attention in class.  Midterm and final were both really easy, just know everything on the study guide, and you're bound to get an A- or higher.  Assignments are a bit trickier, but not hard!  Good prof

Amazing prof! So clear, makes class really, really pleasant, makes you actually want to attend. Gives a summary of previous class before class, this is very helpful! She knows her stuiff and loves it. If you can TAKE THIS CLASS. Even as an elective. Do it.

Trude is a very helpful professor, and her interest in the German language is very clear, yet she tends to mark too hard...always subtracting points for every little error, and you better bet that you will be tested on EVERYTHING in the book--she doesn't leave anything out.  You must study hard for this class if you want to do well.
A true teacher. It doesn't get any better than this.
She creates a great class environment, she's clear and concise when teaching, a fair marker. One of the best profs overall, in my opinion.

She's fair and clear but too strict at marking sometimes. I took German 104 with her and she gives more workload than other prof who taught this class before. However, detail lectures for Ling though!
She is an awesome german teacher. Very fair. Understands her material very well, and is great at helping you understand the material!

She gave us lots of research journal articles. However, she solely tested the lecture notes. I spent a lot of time reading the articles but none of them were in the tests. A classmate seldom attended classes but memorized each point in the notes and Aced it. Who was the genuine learner?
she's very nice and helpful. do the assignments yourself, study the study guide and you'll do fine.
She is the fairest prof ever.  She is remarkable!  Pay attention to the study guides and you'll do fine!
No Comments
Content is hard but the professor repeats a lot which helped me to understand. The assignments were also clear and not tricky. Trude can make a technical class seem easy. She should teach math.
